headline: All majors below the 200-day, funding mixed, whales selling into exchanges while spot ETF inflows persist.
paragraphs: The regime is bear. Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, XRP, BNB, Doge, and Cardano are all trading below their 200-day simple moving averages, and none of them have a golden cross to suggest a trend reversal in progress. Bitcoin sits 46.8% below its 12-month high at 66,383; Ethereum is 60.2% down from its peak; Solana has fallen 68.5%. This is uniform weakness across the board, not selective deleveraging. The crowd knows it: the Fear and Greed gauge is at 25, labeled Extreme Fear, and its 30-day range of 11 to 29 shows that panic has been the regime for weeks. Sentiment has rarely ventured into neutral territory.

Momentum is inconsistent with the downtrend, which is the day's real tension. Bitcoin's RSI-14 is at 61.1 and Ethereum's is at 64.0, both in overbought territory on the daily, while their MACD lines are above their signal lines, suggesting upward momentum. Yet neither asset is above its 200-day average. This divergence, momentum rising while the intermediate trend remains broken, is what technicians call a potential capitulation bottom, but it is not a confirmation: it is a setup, not a signal. Solana's RSI is only 54.5 and its MACD is below signal, so momentum there is weaker. Cardano shows RSI of 55.0 with MACD above signal, but 71.7% annualized volatility suggests the move is noise on a weak structure. The volatility regime is hot: Bitcoin at 32.3%, Ethereum at 45.7%, Solana at 48.4%, and Cardano at 71.7%. In a downtrend with high volatility, momentum divergences can evaporate as quickly as they form.

Positioning on the leverage side reveals crowding in the smaller names and capitulation in Bitcoin shorts. Bitcoin funding on OKX is at 0.0046% per 8 hours, annualizing to 5.0%, which is moderate and not expensive enough to sustain a rally without fresh buyers. Open interest of 2.16 billion USD is substantial but not excessive. The long-to-short ratio is 1.02, nearly flat, so leverage is balanced. However, liquidations in the past 23 hours show 11.65 million USD of shorts liquidated against only 912,000 USD of longs, a 12-to-1 ratio that suggests short-covering or squeeze mechanics rather than fresh conviction in the upside. Ethereum shows a similar pattern: 9.96 million USD of shorts liquidated versus 3.08 million USD of longs. Solana and XRP, much smaller markets, show even more extreme ratios: XRP has 2.27 long-to-short leverage with 598,710 USD of shorts liquidated and only 13,821 USD of longs. These are signs of positions unwinding under duress, not new capital committing to a direction. Doge funding is elevated at 1.0% per 8 hours, 11.0% annualized, with a 4.64 long-to-short ratio, suggesting real money is crowded on the long side in a speculative name.

Whale flows are moving material size onto exchanges, specifically into OKX and Binance. In the past 24 hours, 171.4 million USD of Bitcoin moved to OKX, 65.6 million to Binance, and 118.5 million USD of USDT also moved to OKX. The net direction is negative 237 million USD for Bitcoin: whales are sellers or repositioners, not accumulators. The weekly net flow trend over the past four weeks reads 1.58 billion, 1.39 billion, negative 217 million, and negative 263 million, a clear deterioration from inflows to outflows. Stablecoin float sits at 306.3 billion USD, down 2.06% in 30 days, so dry powder is declining. This combination, selling pressure from large holders plus shrinking cash on the sidelines, is not the posture of a market accumulating strength.

Spot ETF flows into Bitcoin and Ethereum are positive, however: BTC saw 226.8 million USD of net inflow on July 20, and ETH saw 38 million USD. Cumulative flows are 51.6 billion USD for Bitcoin and 11.1 billion USD for Ethereum, substantial institutional commitment. This is the counterweight to the whale selling: traditional finance is buying while holders of size are distributing. Total market cap rose 1.24% in 24 hours to 2.34 trillion USD, and Bitcoin dominance is at 56.8%, so the market is not in freefall. Bitcoin network fees are low at 1 to 2 satoshis per byte, and difficulty rose only 0.8%, so on-chain stress is minimal.

The tension to monitor is whether the momentum divergence holds or snaps. Bitcoin and Ethereum both show RSI overbought but price below the 200-day, which is unsustainable over more than a few days. If Bitcoin reclaims 200-day resistance while spot ETF flows stay positive and whale distribution slows, the setup changes. If whales continue to liquidate into the buying pressure and leverage funding remains moderate, the divergence may simply resolve as a relief bounce into fresh selling. The specific threshold is Bitcoin at its 200-day moving average: a clear hold above that line with sustained funding below 0.01% per 8 hours would be a shift worth tracking. Until then, the tape shows neither a new downtrend nor a confirmed reversal, just a very crowded short being forced to cover while institutions buy below the moving averages.
